title: Animation Steps, after, Pause, Speed, and Cancellation · Language Clinic: Pause Resume Speed
summary: The solver sleeps inside a callback, recursively calls update, schedules duplicate timers, and reset leaves old animation mutating new state. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pause resume speed increment in cancellable event-loop animation scheduler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pause without losing algorithm state, resume once, and apply changed speed to future turns predictably.
Schedule one future callback with after and retain its identifier and owning run generation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-animation-after-cancel-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-animation-after-cancel-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_animation_after_cancel_3(scheduled_steps: int = 42, completed_steps: int = 42, pending_callbacks: int = 0, stale_callbacks: int = 0, pause_stable: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if scheduled_steps < 0 or completed_steps < 0:
        return False, completed_steps, "animation-count"
    if completed_steps != scheduled_steps:
        return False, completed_steps, "missing-step"
    if pending_callbacks > 0 or stale_callbacks > 0:
        return False, pending_callbacks + stale_callbacks, "callback-leak"
    if not pause_stable:
        return False, completed_steps, "pause-mutation"
    assert completed_steps == scheduled_steps and pending_callbacks == 0
    return True, completed_steps, "cancellable-animation"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-pause-resume-speed-1x58xy
# Competency: maze-pause-resume-speed.
# Case 4830: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it.
def evidence_maze_pause_resume_speed_1x58xy(scheduled_steps: int = 42, completed_steps: int = 42, pending_callbacks: int = 0, stale_callbacks: int = 0, pause_stable: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1x58xy = "1x58xy-yx85x1-4830"
    assert evidence_variant_1x58xy
    evidence_axes_1x58xy = (
        "grid1x58xy",
        "topology4830",
        "searchyx85x1",
        "event1x58xy4830",
        "access48301x58xy",
        "recoveryyx85x14830",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1x58xy)) == 6
    if scheduled_steps < 0 or completed_steps < 0:
        return False, completed_steps, "animation-count"
    if completed_steps != scheduled_steps:
        return False, completed_steps, "missing-step"
    if pending_callbacks > 0 or stale_callbacks > 0:
        return False, pending_callbacks + stale_callbacks, "callback-leak"
    if not pause_stable:
        return False, completed_steps, "pause-mutation"
    assert completed_steps == scheduled_steps and pending_callbacks == 0
    return True, completed_steps, "cancellable-animation"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Pause Resume Speed: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 9371 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to model bounded scheduled animation with pause, changed speed, cancellation, and stale-work rejection; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Pause without losing algorithm state, resume once, and apply changed speed to future turns predictably.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Pause Resume Speed model · Theory Pause Resume Speed.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Pause Resume Speed model · Theory Pause Resume Speed, keep Pause Resume Speed connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Pause without losing algorithm state, resume once, and apply changed speed to future turns predictably.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Pause Resume Speed model · Theory Pause Resume Speed, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Pause Resume Speed precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Pause Resume Speed model · Theory Pause Resume Speed, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 9371 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to model bounded scheduled animation with pause, changed speed, cancellation, and stale-work rejection; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Pause without losing algorithm state, resume once, and apply changed speed to future turns predictably.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Pause Resume Speed model · Theory Pause Resume Speed, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Changing a speed variable updates callbacks already queued.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-pause-resume-speed-1x58xy.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-pause-resume-speed-1x58xy(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_pause_resume_speed_1x58xy\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?scheduled_steps < 0 or completed_steps < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?completed_steps != scheduled_steps)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?pending_callbacks > 0 or stale_callbacks > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not pause_stable)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
